key_insight: Plasma-sprayed nano Al2O3–TiO2 coatings improved corrosion resistance on AISI 1020 steel, with the 4 wt.% Al2O3 + 2 wt.% TiO2 coating performing best.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing thermal-spray coating composition can substantially reduce corrosion in mild-steel components exposed to saline, acidic, or temperature-variable environments.
method: Definitive Screening Design (DSD) varied Al2O3 content, NaCl concentration, exposure time, temperature, and pH.
Corrosion performance was measured using potentiodynamic polarization and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS).
A Corrtest CS350M workstation was used to quantify corrosion current, potential, and resistance.
evidence_signal: The best coating reached a corrosion current density of 2.18 µA cm−2 and a minimum corrosion rate of 0.010 mm yr−1, with model fits exceeding R² > 0.99.
watch_item: The reported optimization is specific to the tested plasma-sprayed composition and electrolyte conditions, so broader durability and long-term service performance still need validation.
paper_title: STUDY ON THE CORROSION BEHAVIOUR OF PLASMA SPRAYED NANO Al2O3–TiO2– AISI 1020 STEEL COATINGS USING DEFINITIVE SCREENING DESIGN
